lede: A look at January 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kevin Costner and Chun Doo-hwan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tlahuelilpan pipeline explosion
events.desc: An oil pipeline explosion near Tlahuelilpan, Hidalgo, Mexico, kills 137 people.

events.title: Samara
events.desc: A bus catches fire on the Samara–Shymkent road in Yrgyz District, Aktobe, Kazakhstan. The fire kills 52 passengers, with three passengers and two drivers escaping.

The strongest storm in the United Kingdom in 17 years kills 14 people and Germany sees the worst storm since 1999 with 13 deaths. Cyclone Kyrill causes at least 44 deaths across 20 countries in Western Europe.

events.title: International Olympic Committee
events.desc: The International Olympic Committee restores Jim Thorpe's Olympic medals to his family.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: A Disengagement of Forces agreement is signed between the Israeli and Egyptian governments, ending conflict on the Egyptian front of the Yom Kippur War.

events.title: Willie O'Ree
events.desc: Willie O'Ree, the first Black Canadian National Hockey League player, makes his NHL debut with the Boston Bruins.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British troops launch a general counter-offensive against Italian East Africa.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Paris Peace Conference opens in Versailles, France.

events.title: Field hockey
events.desc: Modern field hockey is born with the formation of The Hockey Association in England.

events.title: Leo II (emperor)
events.desc: Seven-year-old Leo II succeeds his maternal grandfather Leo I as Byzantine emperor. He dies ten months later.

births.name: Chun Doo-hwan
births.line: South Korean general and politician, 5th President of South Korea

births.name: Edmund Barton
births.line: Australian judge and politician, 1st Prime Minister of Australia

births.name: Dave Bautista
births.line: American wrestler, mixed martial artist, and actor

deaths.text: Harivansh Rai Bachchan, Indian poet and author
Adolf Butenandt, German biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Rudyard Kipling, English author and poet, Nobel Prize laureate
John Tyler, American soldier, lawyer, and politician, 10th President of the United States
Louis IX, Duke of Bavaria, Duke of Bavaria-Landshut from 1450 to 1479

observances.text: Christian feast day: Amy Carmichael (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Athanasius of Alexandria (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Confession of Peter (Eastern Orthodox, some Anglican and Lutheran Churches)
